content_text:
As part of our Viable Pathways to Prosperity initiative, we’re #hiring two new roles to help expand career-connected learning, apprenticeship pathways, and economic opportunity for young people across Hamilton County:

☑️ Viable Pathways Operations Manager
📝 Viable Pathways Advisor

These positions will support a new national skilled trades initiative focused on connecting students to high-demand, thriving-wage careers through strong partnerships across education, workforce, labor, and industry.

If you’re passionate about expanding educational opportunity, workforce development, and helping young people build meaningful futures, visit the link below to learn more and apply by June 9: https://lnkd.in/eFXHayxt

#PathwaysToProsperity #OperationsManager

Confidence can be transformative. Our signature professional attire and styling services, paired with interview preparation and confidence coaching, equip women to present themselves with confidence and authenticity as they step into new opportunities. 

Dress for Success Chattanooga delivers more than a moment of support – we provide a comprehensive community of resources and services. We recognize that economic mobility is not achieved through a single intervention, but through sustained, integrated support over time. 

We DREAM for Success, DRESS for Success, DEVELOP for Success, DRIVE for Success, and DONATE for Success. 

Join Us in Igniting Careers and Transforming: donate at www.dfschattanooga.org

The Learning Agility Accelerator is back! We’re excited to launch another cohort of the Learning Agility Accelerator! LAA is a FREE professional development program designed for entry- to mid-level professionals in the Chattanooga area looking to strengthen their digital and workplace technology skills. Over this 15-hour, 3-week experience, participants will learn practical skills for using AI and other digital tools in the workplace. Participants who successfully complete the program will receive a FREE computer. 📍 Lupton Building – 1010 Georgia Ave, Floor 3, Chattanooga, TN 📅 Tuesdays & Thursdays | 5:30 PM – 8:30 PM 🗓️ July 7 – July 21, 2026 Apply here: https://forms.gle/UmrDsSK4T6nWi5vL7 Spots are limited — we’re excited to continue helping Chattanooga professionals build confidence with technology and prepare for the evolving digital workplace.

When people think about healthcare careers, nursing often comes to mind first—but there are many ways to make a difference in healthcare. From fast-paced hospitals to small private offices, healthcare careers offer a wide variety of work environments and opportunities. This summer, explore healthcare career paths at ChattState info sessions for: 🩺 EMS 💙 Nursing (RN) 💊 Pharmacy Tech 🦷 Dental Hygiene 🐾 Veterinary Tech ☢️ Nuclear Medicine Learn about program requirements, career opportunities, and how you can build a future helping others.  Get more info on all Nursing and Allied Health programs at https://ow.ly/JclG50Z2bJ2

We're Hiring! Grants Specialist. Apply for the position in the link below. 

https://lnkd.in/eqxmvku7

Do you believe every child deserves to learn how to read—and that well-supported tutors can help make that possible?

Chattanooga 2.0 is #hiring a Program Manager for an AmeriCorps-funded early literacy tutoring program that places trained tutors in some of Hamilton County’s highest-need elementary schools.

The AmeriCorps program manager will support AmeriCorps volunteers, coordinate across schools, track implementation closely, and help ensure our students receive consistent, high-quality reading support every day.

For more information and how to apply, visit: https://lnkd.in/ehCsQJ4Y

#AmeriCorps #VolunteerTennessee

This project is funded under a Grant Contract with the State of Tennessee, Volunteer Tennessee.

From the classroom to the trails, UTC students are helping care for Chattanooga’s outdoor spaces. 🌲🥾 Students in UTC’s new Trail Building and Management course are gaining hands-on experience by clearing trails, removing hazards and helping restore access to local recreation areas throughout the community. The course combines outdoor recreation, conservation and real-world workforce training while helping meet a growing need for trail management and stewardship in Chattanooga. Read more: https://blog.utc.edu/…/the-chattanooga-trails-utc-program-…/
Calling all 16-21 year olds interested in podcasting, communications, interview skills and audio engineering! A few spots left! Don’t miss this FREE session! 🎙️

Every woman deserves the chance to walk into her future with confidence. 

We’re helping women rewrite their stories through professional attire, career development, support, and empowerment. Your donation can help a woman prepare for an interview, gain confidence, and step into a new chapter of success.

This is more than clothing.
This is confidence.
This is opportunity.
This is HER STORY.

Donate today and help us continue changing lives in our community.

#HelpHerStory #DressForSuccess #DressForSuccessChattanooga #SuccessStartsNow

Your Future Starts Here! 🚀 Join us for our Open House at Miller-Motte College! Explore our labs, experience our "Touch the Trade" activities, and see what hands-on career training is all about. 🛠️⚡ 📆 When: Friday, June 12, 2026 | 3:00 PM – 6:00 PM 📍 Where: Jacksonville Campus (105 New Frontier Way, Jacksonville, NC) Bonus: Refreshments while supplies last! What to Expect: 🏢 Tour the campus & training spaces  👨‍🏭 Meet our expert, real-world instructors  🎓 Get answers about Admissions, Financial Aid, & Career Services  Featured Programs: 🚛 CDL Training: Class A Tractor Trailer 🏗️ Construction & Trades Management ⚡ Electrical ⚙️ HVAC & Basic Refrigeration 🩺 Medical Clinical Assistant 🚰 Plumbing Whether you're a recent high school grad or looking for a change, come find out where you'll land! Everyone is welcome. 🔗 Click the link to RSVP and learn more! https://www.miller-motte.edu/events/jacksonville-open-house/

🚨 Countdown to TOMORROW’S City Center Expo is ON! 🚨 Get ready to connect, collaborate, and celebrate the incredible businesses that make Chattanooga thrive!  The City Center Expo is your opportunity to: 💡 Discover amazing local businesses 🤝 Expand your professional network 💬 Meet new faces and make valuable connections 🔗 Get plugged into Chattanooga’s vibrant business community FREE TO ATTEND! Whether you’re a business owner, entrepreneur, professional, or community supporter, this is the place to be. Don’t miss an evening full of networking, innovation, and local pride! We can’t wait to see you there!  When: May 21, 11:30AM-1:00PM Where: The Historic - 1601 Rossville Avenue

Turns out play really is for adults too!

Our YPC Members Only night at the Creative Discovery Museum was such a fun one. Between exploring the interactive exhibits, reconnecting with familiar faces, meeting new people, and getting surprisingly competitive over Networking Bingo, there was something happening in every corner of the museum.

Members also got to tap into their creative side with string art woodworking projects — creating custom designs with yarn, nails, and wood to take home at the end of the night.

And shoutout to Binh and Rogelio for taking home the Networking Bingo wins and their $50 gift cards courtesy of Frazier Five & Dime. Thanks for helping make the night even more fun.

Thanks to everyone who came out and made this members-only event such a great time!

We had a great time with the Catoosa County Chamber of Commerce this morning! It was a pleasure to host Cards and Coffee. 💙What a great Cards & Coffee event! We hope you all had an amazing time! Thank you to Goodwill Industries of the Greater Chattanooga Area for hosting us and providing breakfast and coffee! #CatoosaConnects #Networking

content_text:
DJ Khaled voice: “Anotha one.” 📢  

We’re excited to share another opportunity with the Southeast Tennessee Area Agency on Aging & Disability. We’re hiring a Quality Assurance Coordinator to support state and federally funded programs across our 10-county service area.

This role is ideal for someone who is organized, analytical, proactive, and passionate about supporting quality services for older adults and individuals with disabilities. Responsibilities include provider monitoring, site visits, technical assistance, satisfaction survey coordination, reporting, and supporting continuous quality improvement initiatives.

Qualifications:
✔ Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university OR equivalent work-related experience
✔ Strong written and verbal communication skills
✔ Ability to maintain confidentiality
✔ Ability to work independently and collaboratively

📍 Position serves Southeast Tennessee’s 10-county region

📅 Applications must be submitted by Friday, June 5, 2026

To apply, please send your resume and cover letter to: Don Kellerman at dkellerman@sedev.org

Or mail to:
ATTN: Human Resources
P.O. Box 4757
Chattanooga, TN 37405

The Southeast Tennessee Development District is an equal opportunity employer/program. Auxiliary aids and services are available upon request to individuals with disabilities. Tennessee Relay 711.

content_text: Open House TODAY  NEW ChattState Dayton May 19, 4 – 6 pm 815 Delaware Ave., Dayton, TN Audio described video: https://youtu.be/YhrIv_LZWGI?si=6h-dkVDtrF6tef_w

“I identify with Goodwill’s guiding principle that says, ‘Every time we touch a donation, something good should happen,’” said Katie, Goodwill Learning and Development Training Coordinator. For Katie, this “something good” is reflected not only in her own career growth, but in the success of the Goodwill team members she now trains. Before working at Goodwill, Katie earned a degree in animal science and worked in several industries, including food services. After she and her family moved from Kentucky to the Chattanooga area, she stayed at home to care for her two children for several years. When she decided to return to the workforce, she faced a familiar challenge that many caregivers encounter: a gap in employment and uncertainty about where to begin. “My mom is a store associate at a Goodwill in Kentucky, and she told me about all the wonderful opportunities there,” said Katie. “I thought since it is such a great thing, maybe I could be a part of it too.” Katie applied for a position at the Goodwill store and donation center in Fort Oglethorpe, Georgia. Recognizing her potential, Christy, the Director of Retail Operations, offered her a shift lead position with clear opportunities for growth. Within three months, Katie was promoted to Assistant Manager. After three more months, she was promoted to Store Manager at our new, much larger Fort Oglethorpe store.  While enjoying store leadership, Katie found herself drawn back to a skill she discovered earlier in her career, training others. “When I worked in food services, I realized how much I enjoyed teaching and helping people grow. When I saw the Goodwill Learning and Development Training Coordinator job posting, it was truly an ‘aha’ moment.” Katie now serves as our Learning and Development Training Coordinator and says the role feels like a perfect fit. She shared, “I’ve found my passion again.” When you donate or shop at Goodwill, you’re doing more than decluttering or finding a great deal; you’re investing in people like Katie. Your support fuels career pathways and leadership development right here in our community. Our mission is to help people transform potential into opportunity, and our vision is a community where everyone can thrive. It’s more than Goodwill; it’s good work. #GoodwillChatt

content_text:
Big things happen when community leaders come together with a shared purpose. 

Today, the fabulous Ms. Toni and Dr. Robin Sturnes attended the Rotary Club meeting, where Dress for Success Chattanooga was honored to receive grant funding to continue helping women on their journey to economic mobility and career success.

Partnerships like these create real impact in our community helping women gain confidence, resources, professional development, and opportunities for a brighter future.

Thank you to the Chattanooga Rotary Club for believing in the mission and investing in women who are ready to thrive. ✨

#DressForSuccess #SuccessStartsNow #Chattanooga #CommunityImpact

Recently, the Office of Community Health held its annual job fair at Carver Community Center. Job seekers met with hiring companies to explore employment opportunities in our area. We appreciate the companies that partnered with us to help increase employment opportunities for Chattanooga residents!

Your future starts with one visit 🙌 Join us at the #MMCAugusta Explore Your Future Open House and get a firsthand look at hands-on training, career-focused programs, and the support designed to help you succeed 💙 Tour the campus, explore training labs, meet instructors and campus leadership, and learn more about programs like Combination Welding, Electrical, HVAC, CDL Training, IT Support Specialist, Healthcare Information Technology, and more 🔧⚡🚛💻 🗓 Saturday, May 16, 2026 ⏰ 11:00 AM – 1:00 PM 📍 3128 Deans Bridge Rd, Suite A, Augusta, GA Whether you’re ready to enroll or just starting to explore your options, this is the perfect chance to take the next step toward your future. Plus, enjoy refreshments and enter for a chance to win door prizes while supplies last 🎉 Learn more and RSVP here: https://www.miller-motte.edu/…/mmc-augusta-explore-your-fu…/

The countdown is on! #MMCRaleigh is gearing up for an exciting day of hands-on competition, skilled trades action, and campus exploration this Friday at our Weld Wars & Brazing Wars event! ⚡🛠️ Join us at 9:00 AM to cheer on competitors as they put their welding and brazing skills to the test, then stick around for our Open House from 3:00 PM – 6:00 PM to tour the campus, explore our training programs, meet the team, and experience what Miller-Motte College is all about. 🙌 Whether you're curious about a future in the skilled trades or just want to see the action up close, we’d love to see you there! 📍 Learn more here: https://ow.ly/N4Y950YYr2G

These ladies all came together for the Women's Fund of Greater Chattanooga luncheon this week at Embassy Suites downtown Chattanooga to hear the incredible Janet Rehberg, PMP, CEM speak and share her insights on leadership and innovation. An amazing Sold Out event and they filled our ballroom (almost 200 ladies!!) Such an inspirational afternoon with all of these women who are shaping Chattanooga’s future!! Melody Shekari it was an honor to have you all here!  
#chattanooga #inspirationalwomen #ladyleadership #womennetworking

I enjoyed attending the Women’s Leadership Collaborative event today, which brought together more than 190 women.

The CEO of EPB, Janet Rehberg, PMP, CEM , spoke about emotional intelligence and leadership, sharing several key takeaways:

- Surround yourself with men and women who will advocate for you when you are not present.
- Communication is one of the most impactful skills one can possess.
- Networking is not just about who you know, but about who knows you. Engage in networking with intentionality.

Thank you to Janet and everyone involved in organizing this amazing event.

📣 We’re Hiring! 📣
The Southeast Tennessee Development District Area Agency on Aging & Disability is seeking a Home Delivered Meals Site Coordinator to serve the Monteagle and Tracy City areas.

This position plays an important role in supporting older adults in our communities by coordinating and overseeing home delivered meal services. Responsibilities include managing daily meal operations, maintaining accurate reporting, ensuring compliance with program guidelines, and working closely with volunteers and community partners.

🕘 Hours: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM
We’re looking for someone who is:
✔ Organized and dependable
✔ A strong communicator
✔ Able to work independently and manage multiple tasks
✔ Passionate about serving older adults and the community
Experience working with older adults or in a community-based setting is preferred.

📅 Application Deadline: Friday, May 15, 2026
📧 Send resumes, cover letter, and references to: Crystal Scogin at  cscogin@sedev.org

📬 Or mail to:
Southeast Tennessee Development District
P.O. Box 4757
Chattanooga, TN 37405
ATTN: Human Resources

📞 Questions? Call 423-424-4279
The Southeast Tennessee Development District is an equal opportunity employer/program. Auxiliary aids and services are available upon request to individuals with disabilities. Tennessee Relay 711.

The Heavy Equipment Operator's Course FALL COHORT is Registering NOW! 

Chattanooga State’s Heavy Equipment Operator Course is preparing individuals with the skills employers are actively seeking—and the results speak for themselves. Several Chattanooga-area construction companies are already offering job possibilities to students upon successful completion of the program. 
Find more course information here: https://lnkd.in/dEFPq7N

This course is designed to provide practical, workforce-ready training!

Those concerned about cost; funding assistance may be available through the American Job Center for eligible participants.

If you or someone you know is interested in:
Entering the construction industry
Advancing current skills
Connecting training directly to employment opportunities
I am happy to help guide you through the next steps and discuss funding options.

Contact: Kristi Mead
 Program & Events Coordinator, Economic & Workforce Development
 Chattanooga State Community College
Email: kristi.mead@chattanoogastate.edu Phone: (423)697-2514

Invest in skills that lead to opportunity—right here in our community.
